name: escape-the-dungeon-gad-emergent
description: GAD+Emergent combined condition: agent gets the full .planning/ XML scaffold AND GAD framework skills AND inherited skills from prior emergent runs. The maximally-scaffolded condition. Tests whether combining framework planning + methodology skills + inherited project-specific skills produces the best outcomes — or whether the overhead of ALL that context drowns the agent. If this beats both GAD-alone and Emergent-alone, it validates the emergent-evolution hypothesis (gad-68) in its strongest form: fundamentals + inheritance + planning = mastery. If it underperforms, the overhead hypothesis gets another data point: more scaffolding ≠ better output.

dimensions.label: Playability
dimensions.description: Does the game run end-to-end? Title → new game → rooms → combat → return → continue. No softlocks.

dimensions.label: UI polish
dimensions.description: Does the UI feel intentional? Icons, HP/mana bars, room-type differentiation, styled controls.

dimensions.label: Mechanics implementation
dimensions.description: Are combat, forge, rune crafting, resistances functional and coherent?

dimensions.label: Ingenuity requirement met
dimensions.description: v4+ principle: do starter abilities feel insufficient? Is the forge necessary, not cosmetic?

dimensions.label: Stability
dimensions.description: Crashes? Stale state? Save/load works? Finish a run without reload?

dimensions.label: Skill inheritance effectiveness
dimensions.description: GAD+EMERGENT-SPECIFIC: did the agent productively apply BOTH GAD framework skills AND inherited emergent skills? Did it use GAD skills (plan-phase, execute-phase, verify-phase) to structure its work AND inherited skills (game-loop-verification, state-composition) to improve its implementation? Did the two skill sets interact or conflict?
